comment: The procedural draft: the noise-driven base world Terrain.height() builds UNDER the blessed Strata tile (which replaces it where present). Valley's archipelago, moved out of game/world/terrain.gd's VALLEY_PATH/WALL_HEIGHT/SEABED consts and hardcoded noise seeds per FW4. A content-empty game ships no landform and falls back to terrain.gd's neutral defaults (no home valley, flat walls, generic noise). The draft's fixed amplitude profile (floor/wall/range/seabed multipliers) is the interpreter's shape and stays in terrain.gd + the native kernel — this record carries only the per-game parameters the kernel already takes via set_base/set_home.

valley.comment: Home valley centerline (behind spawn, past the pond, to the shrine); floor stays low and dense, walls rise into an enclosing ridge plateau.
